summarization.a: The figure presents stacked XRD diffraction patterns comparing LiMn₂O₄ and Li–MnO₂ thin films before and after annealing at 600 °C. The changes in peak positions and intensities reveal phase evolution, including the formation of the lithiated spinel LiMn₂O₄ and the disappearance of β-MnO₂ reflections upon heating.

c.question: What crystalline phase appears after annealing Li–MnO₂ films?
What structural insights can be derived from these XRD patterns?
c.answer: β-MnO₂ to LiMn₂O₄ transformation, New diffraction peaks, Reduced β-MnO₂ reflections.

a.question: How does annealing at 600 °C affect the XRD patterns of Li–MnO₂ films?
a.answer: Annealing induces the transformation of β-MnO₂ to the spinel LiMn₂O₄ phase, as indicated by the emergence of new diffraction peaks and the reduction of β-MnO₂ reflections. This demonstrates increased crystallinity and lithium incorporation upon heat treatment.

a.question: Does the annealing process at 600 °C alter the crystalline structure of Li–MnO₂?
